2 . W e r ecommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal Treasurer and the following:
a) Municipal Treasurer
a.1 Abide to Section 122 of the Presidential Decree (P.D) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.
a.2 Prepare, update and submit the Bond, Statement of Assets, Liabilities and Networth (SALN), Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) and Report of Accountability for Accountable Form (RAAF).
a.3 Submit the Bond, Statement of Assets, Liabilities and Networth (SALN), Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) and Report of Accountability for Accountable Form (RAAF).
b) Municipal Accountant
b.1 Abide to Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of the COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
b.2 Prepare the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS).
b.3 Submit to the Audit Team the BRS together with the Bank Statements, Deposit Slips, Debit and Credit Memos and the Subsidiary Ledger of the Municipal Treasurer.

6. We recommend that the Bid and Award Committee and other concerned personnel abide to Section 3.1.1 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-001 and provide the audit team of the contracts, purchase order and corresponding supporting documents within five (5) days after perfection. Moreover, we recommend the management to request for technical inspection by the Regional Technical Office to ensure the regularity and propriety of the contracts.
9) Submit a written justification explaining therein the reasons for processing claims without complete documentation.

20) Invite representative from the Commission on Audit (COA) to act as witness in an inventory-taking to comply with the afore-cited regulation.
21) Invitation shall be given at least three days prior to the scheduled date to give the auditor an ample time to prepare and settle whatever time inconveniences that may have.

60) We recommended to the head of agency to instruct Municipal Accountant to issue certification stating therein that previous cash advance has been liquidated and accounted for in the books of accounts in support to additional claim of cash advance together with other liquidation documents; otherwise, a grant of additional cash advance shall not be processed and approved.

61) We recommended to management and to Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit the required advance copy of procurement documents within the reglementary period to ensure a systematic and effective review process with a view of generating timely and relevant audit results.
62) We further recommended to concerned persons/offices to provide us copies of these documents for monitoring of deliveries and inspection purposes.

63) We recommended to management to require supplier/contractor to post warranty security on completed deliveries/infrastructure projects following the prescribed schedule in section 62.1 and 62.2 of revised IRR of RA 9184 and furnish the auditor of the copy immediately afterwards.

64) The municipality shall maintain special accounts in the general fund specifically for the 20% Development Fund through preparation and maintenance of complete subsidiary ledgers to comply with the afore-mentioned regulations as well as facilitate monitoring and easy preparation of FS for special accounts. Moreover, The Municipal Accountant should prepare FS for special accounts and shall submit the same to COA as an attachment to the FS for General Fund.

65) The municipal accountant should be required to record all consummated procurement thru the inventory account and properly follow the steps of recording incoming and out-going supplies and materials in a perpetual method of inventory keeping. This will prevent purchases in bulk be charged to outright expense; and
66) Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer must maintain Stock Cards as required for in Section 119 of NGAS Manual Vol. I for local government.

1.10 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
a) Abide Section 57 of RA No. 464, otherwise known as the Real
Property Tax Code, Section 4 of RA No. 5447, otherwise known as
An Act Creating Special Education Fund and Section 470 (d) (2) of
RA No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of
1991.
b) Make remedial measures in order to collect the long standing
receivables.

2.7 We recommended to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to:
a) Comply Section V of the COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated
November 23, 2015.
b) Establish an Inventory Committee in order to conduct an inventory
of the road networks and prepare a report (Refer to Annex C);
c) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to update the Local Road
Networks based on the result of the physical count of the Inventory
Committee and disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements. (Refer
to Annex B).
d) Recognize the Head of the GSO to maintain the Local Road
Networks Map and prepare a report (Refer to Annex A) and also
maintain the Local Road Network Property Card (Refer to Annex
E)
c) Direct the Municipal Engineer to furnish the Municipal Accountant
and Head of the GSO the complete description and of the Local road
projects.

3.6 In this regard, we recommended that the LCE:
a) Abide to Sections 4.2 and 4.2.2 of Annex A under the COA
Resolution No. 2021-010 dated May 18, 2021;
b) Establish a Disposal Committee for that purpose,
c) Execute the disposal process by the Disposal Committee and
d) Make the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of
accounts by the Municipal Accountant.

4.7 We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator (MPDC) to submit the required documents for
legal and technical reviews and inspection by the TAD, Commission on
Audit-BARMM in accordance with Sections 18 (4) and 122 of PD 1445.

5.4 In this regard, we, therefore, recommended that the Honorable Mayor
instruct the personnel concerned to settle their respective Notice of
Suspension, in accordance with Section 9.4 of the COA Circular No. 2009-
006 dated September 15, 2009.

1.11 We recommended that Management:
a) Instruct the inventory committee to prioritize and take charge the overall
conduct of physical count of PPE to prove the existence of the recorded
agency’s properties and require them to prepare the corresponding Report on
Physical Count of Property Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) with appropriate
remarks on the condition and whereabouts of the PPE.
b) Direct the accounting and property offices to maintain and update complete
PPELC and PC, respectively, per class of PPE to facilitate the reconciliation
of balances with the results of inventory taking and copy furnish the office of
the auditor with accurate descriptions and details not later than January 31 of
each year.
c) Invite representative from COA to act as witness in every physical
count, and the letter-request shall be served and stamped received by COA at
least three days prior to the scheduled date.
d) Report all obsolete and unserviceable PPEs in the Inventory and Inspection
Report on Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) duly signed by the head of the
accounting unit, inspection /appraisal committee and approved by the head of
the agency or authorized representative, and submit the report to COA
together with other required documents for the necessary inspection and
validation.
e) Disclose in the notes to FS the carrying amounts of PPE retired from active
use and held for disposal so as not to misled users of the FS, and upon
disposal, draw a JEV to derecognize the unserviceable properties from the
accounting and property records.
f) Direct property officer to facilitate issuance and/or renewal of Property
Acknowledgement Reports (PARs) every three years to ensure accountability
over government properties.

2.8 We recommended that Management:
a) Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the required certified list of
taxpayers with the amount of taxes due and collectible, and submit the same
to the Municipal Accountant on a yearly basis in order to reflect correct
RPT/SET receivables at the beginning of the year, and maintain close
coordination with the Municipal Assessor as regards to the newly assessed
real properties and require the Municipal Assessor to submit an assessment
roll every end of year.
b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to
adjust the amount of recorded receivable to reconcile with the GL balance
and the amount that is actually collectible for each taxpayer, if the required
certified list of taxpayers is already made available.

3.8 We recommended that Management undertake the following:
a) Require concerned officials to be more careful in its budget preparation
particularly on income estimation.
b) Develop and recommend measures that will identify and address issues
in budget planning.
c) Adopt measures in order to improve collections and strictly enforce
collection strategies as required in the afore-mentioned laws and
regulations.

4.15 We hereby recommended that the LCE undertake the following:
a. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to impose internal rules in the movement or
flow of transactions, like the claiming of checks, in order to expedite
submission of reports and documents to the Municipal Accountant for
submission of the latter to the auditor.
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to transmit immediately all un-submitted
DVs and other financial reports to allow us review and evaluate said reports;
and deficiencies noted, if there is any, could be communicated to
management for its immediate and appropriation action.
c. Comply with the required timeframe on the rendition of accounts
including preparation and submission of financial reports and so timely
review and verification could be made.
d. Require officials concerned to submit justification letter explaining therein
the reasons for late submission and/or impose appropriate sanction against
those officers/employees concerned who remained unmindful of the deadlines
on the submission of accounts and reports

5.10 We recommended that the management:
a) Prepare and issue policy guidelines to address the issues on the necessity
and propriety of hiring of JOs/Casuals.
b) Re-evaluate and reassess the hiring of JOs and determine if their
services are necessary and supportive of the agency’s mission and vision,
and for the improvement of their overall performance as local
government unit, otherwise, consider termination of the contract of JOs
whose services is just a duplication of the function of regular employees
and/or other Job Order personnel.
c) Hiring of JOs purposely to augment existing plantilla and/or job to be
performed are essential and necessary where there are not enough
regular staff to meet the demands of the service shall be supported with
a CSC duly attested appointment.
d) Computation of the JOs wages shall be on a daily basis excluding
Saturdays, Sundays and holidays and the services shall not be
continuous as termination of JOs is needed immediately upon expiration
of the six month period.
e) Instruct all JOs/Casuals to prepare DTRs and other necessary
supporting documents in support of the payment of their salary/wages.
f) Submit JO contracts to COA on a timely manner as before the effectivity
of the contract.
g) Revisit pertinent laws and regulations on the hiring, supervision and
payment of JOs.

6.7 We recommended that Management:
a) Faithfully hire Consultants which are project-based for agency’s
priority projects and programs and to be terminated upon completion of said
projects or programs.
b) Thoroughly review the qualification and function of the consultants,
thru the Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) and the Bids and
Awards Committee (BAC) to ascertain whether their services are
necessary and in support to the goals and objectives of the Municipality. In
case there is an inevitable need for hiring them, engage only experts in their
respective fields and observe the rules and regulations on the hiring of
consultants pursuant to RA 9184.
c) Prepare a consultancy contract instead of a JO contract for
consultants and submit the same to COA on a timely basis.
d) Prepare written policy for the hiring of consultants and furnish a copy to
the office of the auditor.e) Require all consultants to submit monthly accomplishment report to
support the payment of their claim.f) Revisit pertinent laws and regulations on the hiring, supervision and
payment of consultants.

7.12 We hereby recommended that Management:
a) Require the BAC to observe the provisions stated in the 2016 RIRR of
R.A. No. 9184 to prevent audit suspension and/or disallowance and the most
advantageous price in favor of the Municipal Government can be availed of.
b) Require the BAC Secretariat to submit proof of posting of NOA, NTP and the
approved Contract in the PhilGEPS, procuring entity websites, if there is any,
and in any conspicuous places in the premises of the procuring entity.
c) Submit all the lacking documents to settle all audit suspensions relative to
the above-mentioned deficiency.
d) Submit written justification explaining therein the reasons for non-
posting of NOA, NTP and approved Contract.

8.14 We hereby recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Engineer or
official concerned to:
a) Properly and correctly prepare list of all on-going government
projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the
year to allow us review and validate entries made in an easy and efficient
manner.
b) Inform the office of the auditor within 10 days after the award of
projects/programs/activities or before its start that the project signboard was
already posted to comply with the afore-cited regulations and enable the
auditor to immediately inform the management of any deficiencies that
could be noted in project implementation.
c) Prepare the PPAs monitoring reports in a manner where all vital
information is present and so validation and monitoring could be easily
done. Likewise, the submission shall be on a monthly basis instead on a
quarterly basis to give us ample time to undertake the necessary review and
validation.

9.6 We hereby recommended that LCE:
a) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit all the lacking documents
within the required timeframe; and ensure that submitted supporting documents
are all original and complete copies of the same and the preparation are in
accordance with the afore-said laws, rules and regulations, and so project
inspection and validation could be promptly undertaken.
b) Direct the Municipal Engineer to monitor the implementation of projects
diligently and provide concerned officials complete and accurate data to
properly documents implementation and enable them to submit required report
reflective of the actual status of its implementation.
c) Require concerned official to comply with the required deadlines of
submission of required documentation to facilitate our review and monitoring
of infrastructure projects.

10.7 We hereby recommended that Management:
a) Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to
prioritize the maintenance and updates of their respective accounting and
budget records, using the prescribed form, and update the same regularly to
ensure accurate and reliable account balances in the Financial Statements.
b) Address the issues on the lack of manpower the soonest possible time by
properly considering the required expertise and qualification including their
attendance to trainings/seminars to keep them abreast of the latest accounting
and budget issuances.

11.8 We hereby recommended the following:
a) Submit the approved APP incorporating therein all procurement
requirements either for infrastructure projects or projects undertaken by
administration including those needed for the implementation of various
programs and activities to be able to present a complete and valid procurement
plan.
b) Use the APP format as prescribed by the GPPB and update the same
regularly to suit the needs of the agency’s procurement.
c) Ensure that all procurement are in accordance with the approved APP.
d) Conduct regular monitoring of projects implemented for the year, and
prepare a progress report copy furnished the office of the auditor. Likewise,
ensures that target date of completion as reflected in the APP/AIP are met,
otherwise, revised APP/AIP will be prepared to reflect the changes made in
the implementation.
e) Ensure that said reports are submitted within the deadline for a timely
evaluation and audit of said projects.

12.5 We hereby recommended that Management:
a) Give importance and prioritize compliance to audit recommendations
incorporated in the AAR and so agency’s overall performance will improve.
b) Take note of the required timeframe for submission of the duly
accomplished AAPSI following the correct format for a timely validation of
the same.
c) Provide copy of the said AAPSI to DBM, Speaker of the House of
Representatives, House Committee on Appropriations and Senate
Committee on Finance as mandated in the above-mentioned law.
d) Posting of the said report in the agency’s website is likewise enjoined.

13.8 We recommended that Management:
a) Immediately settle all audit suspensions to allow us complete our
audit on GAD fund and enable us make prompt decision, and so result of
audit could be communicated to management on a timely manner.
b) Refrain from processing transaction not duly supported with
documents.
c) Ensure that identified GAD projects and activities are responsive to
gender issues of the agency, otherwise, exclude the amount of the
non-GAD related project in the computation of the mandated
appropriation, and consequently, the transaction shall be evaluated
for the issuance of an audit suspension or disallowance, as the case
may warrant.

14.4 We hereby recommended that management:
a) Submit immediately all the lacking documents to enable us review and
validate LDRRM related expenditures; and facilitate completion of the audit
on LDRRM fund.
b) Pay attention of the required deadlines for submission and so post audit
could be promptly undertaken in an extensive and efficient manner.

1. We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Accountant to depreciate the seventeen (17) Property, Plant and Equipment Account (PPE) which were previously depreciated but not on CY 2020 as listed in the Table I and those which were not depreciated at all times with thirteen (13) PPE under Table II and to instruct the Supply Officer to submit to the audit team the Report of the Physical Count on the Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE), pursuant to Section 262 of the Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for Local Government Units, Volume I and Section 124 (2) of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standard (PPSAS) 17 – Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE).

a) Local Chief Executive
a.1 To apply the guidelines and procedures on inventory taking in accordance with Section 3.0 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
a.2 To create the Inventory Committee composing of the Accounting, Property Divisions/Units and Internal Audit Service/Unit for the purpose of cleansing the PPE Account in accordance with Section 5.2 of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

b) Inventory Committee
b.1 To prepare the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) in accordance with Section 5.9 of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
b.2 To conduct the Physical Inventory taking pursuant to Section 5.3 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
b.3 To prepare the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) in pursuant of Section 6.2.13 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

c. Property Unit
c.1 To ensure that the recording are updated pursuant to Section 6.3.1(a) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
c.2 To prepare Property Cards for items found at station, in accordance with Section 6.3.1(c) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
c.3 To prepare list of non existing/missing PPEs in accordance with Section 6.3.1(d) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
c.4 To prepare Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) in accordance with Section 6.3.1(h) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
c.5 To reconcile with the Accounting Unit in accordance with Section 6.3.1(f) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

d) Accounting Unit
d.1 To take necessary accounting entries to recognize PPEs found at station and prepare corresponding Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards based on the List of PPEs found at Station in accordance with Section 6.3.2(a) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
d.2 To take necessary accounting entries to recognize loss of PPE and to set up corresponding receivables from concerned accountable officer pursuant to Section 6.3.2(b) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006.
d.3 To reconcile with the Property Unit, pursuant to Section 6.3.2(c) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
d.4 To take up necessary accounting entries to write-off/drop from the books of accounts the remaining balance as of December 31, 2021, pursuant to Section 6.3.2(e) of COA Circular No. 2020-006.
d.5 To ensure the total balance of PPELCs/SLs totally with the balances of controlling PPE accounts in the General Ledgers, pursuant to Section 6.3.2(f) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

e) Internal Audit Service/Unit
e.1 To witness the conduct of the inventory in accordance with Section 5.5 of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

4. We recommend the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the above personnel mentioned below the following:
a) Supply Officer
a.1 Abide to Section D.7 of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Vehicle.
a.2 Prepare the Monthly Fuel Consumption (MFC).
a.3 Submit to the Auditor the Driver’s Trip Tickets (DTT) and Monthly Fuel Consumption.
b) Drivers
b.1 To adhere to Section D.6 of the above Manual.
b.2 To prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels (MROT) where he summarizes in chronological order his trip for the month.
b.3 To submit the above report to the Chief Accountant.
c) Chief Accountant
c.1 To submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels of the Drivers to the Auditor.

5. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the personnel concerned to settle their respective Notice of Suspension, in accordance to Section 9.4 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

7. We recommend the management to adopt the uniform property identification system in accordance with Sections 5.6 and 5.7 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

1) The head of agency shall direct official concerned to limit payment thru reimbursement basis and comply with the requirement on the principle of imprest system; that all payment must be paid by check except those that cannot be conveniently paid by check, it could be paid thru the cash advance of the Municipal Treasurer.
2) Procurement of office supplies and other recurring expenses of the municipality shall be undertaken in bulk for a maximum of 3 months consumption thru procurement service/public bidding or even thru shopping so as to minimize transactions thru reimbursements.
3) Practice of recording reimbursements in lieu of cash advances and/or vice versa shall be discontinued as this manifestly show the motive of circumventing the law since the payee could not be granted cash advance other than official travel and/or no supporting documents is available to justify the claim thru reimbursement.

2) Procurement of office supplies and other recurring expenses of the municipality shall be undertaken in bulk for a maximum of 3 months consumption thru procurement service/public bidding or even thru shopping so as to minimize transactions thru reimbursements.

3) Practice of recording reimbursements in lieu of cash advances and/or vice versa shall be discontinued as this manifestly show the motive of circumventing the law since the payee could not be granted cash advance other than official travel and/or no supporting documents is available to justify the claim thru reimbursement.

4) Ensure that all procurements shall pass through the regular mode of procurement to promote fair competition in accordance with R.A. 9184. 1. Comply with the requirements on adequate procurement planning to forestall emergency purchases.

5) Procurement of office supplies and other recurring expenses of the Municipality shall be undertaken in bulk for a maximum of 3 months consumption thru procurement service/public bidding or even thru shopping so as to minimize transactions thru reimbursements.
6) Submit justification letter explaining therein the reasons of the said claims despite violation of existing regulation.
7) Municipal Accountant shall likewise ensure that all disbursements are supported with complete documentation.

8) Existing practice of the agency on processing claims not duly supported with documents shall be discontinued as this is not the proper way of processing claims and it may further create adverse impact to agency and to concerned officials if this will continue to exist in their transactions.

10) The Municipal Accountant shall likewise submit all the required documentation needed to establish the validity and legality of the disbursements and henceforth, payment shall only be made for claims supported with complete documentation.

11) The Municipal Treasurer shall render her monthly reports such as Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) with supporting documents, Payrolls, Official Receipts and other liquidation documents including revenue reports and supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant on or before the 5th day of the ensuing month to allow its prompt recording in the books of accounts.
12) The Municipal Accountant shall coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to ensure prompt submission of DVs and other related reports, undertake recording in the books of accounts, and submit the same within 10 days of the ensuing month to the Office of the Auditor.
13) Concerned officials shall prioritize and adopt measures to address issues on delayed submission of reports to be able to meet the deadline set and be free from possible sanctions may be imposed from violating thereof as required under article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.
14) Require concerned official/s to submit written justification explaining therein the reasons for late submission, and if not satisfied, appropriate actions shall be instituted against the accountable officers for the delayed/non submission of financial reports as required under Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.

15) To faithfully comply and enforce the periodic submission of these reports within the prescribed period. Concerned officials shall likewise prioritize submission of these reports and look into the root cause of these delays and immediately initiate measures to address the problem.
16) Require concern official/s to submit written justification explaining therein the reasons of late submission, and if not satisfied, appropriate actions shall be instituted against the accountable officers for the delayed/non submission of financial reports as required under Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.

17) We hereby recommended that management shall cause the preparation and submission of the required Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption to comply with the foregoing regulations, and to facilitate proper control and accounting of the fuel expense; and establish reasonableness of fuel consumption as well.

18) The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer shall exert more efforts to maintain their respective accounting and budget records, using the prescribed form, and update the same regularly to ensure accurate and reliable account balances in the Financial Statements.
19) The necessity of additional workforce, who has the required expertise and qualification, for the said offices shall be properly considered and shall be prioritized as well including their attendance to trainings/seminars to keep them abreast of the latest accounting and budget issuances.

22) Intensify efforts for the timely conduct of annual physical count of PPE and submit the required Reports on PPE to COA within deadline set.
23) Invite representative from COA to witness the said inventory taking. Invitation letter shall be sent three (3) days prior to the scheduled date to give ample time to prepare and settle whatever time inconveniences that may have.

24) Submit all other transaction documents and so we could complete our audit on GAD Funds and refrain from processing GAD transaction not duly supported with documents.
25) Immediately submit all other requirements in settlement of audit suspension to avoid disallowance in audit; and it will facilitate completion of the audit on GAD fund as well.

26) Immediately submit all other documents to settle audit suspension to avoid disallowance in audit; and it will facilitate completion of the audit on LDRRM fund as well.
27) Necessary adjustment shall be made to correct errors in recording thru this entry, viz: DRRM Equipment (1-07-05-090) P300,000.00 Prior Period Adjustment (3-01-01-020) P300,000.00

28) The management shall faithfully follow correct disbursement system and so only valid and proper claims will be processed and paid.
29) Exert all possible efforts to settle all audit suspensions within the required timeframe to avoid disallowance in audit, and the required ocular inspection could be promptly undertaken as well.
30) Be guided of DBM, DILG Joint Memorandum Circular NO. 2017 -1 for authorized projects to be implemented out of the Local Development Fund.

31) The Municipal Accountant shall not process claims/payments not duly supported with required documents. He shall likewise follow correct disbursement system at all times.
32) Exert all possible efforts to settle all audit suspensions within the required timeframe to avoid disallowance in audit.
33) Be guided of the pertinent sections in the afore-mentioned regulations and in other GPPB issued resolutions simplifying the requirements in project implementation during emergency cases.

34) The Municipal Accountant shall prioritize submission of the COVID related reports and transactions documents since the prescribed period within to submit has already lapsed, and the necessity of its immediate audit has been laid down to establish proper utilization thereof.

35) The Accounting, Supply and Property Units shall update regularly, PPE Subsidiary Ledgers, Stock Cards and Property Cards, respectively, and reconcile balances of these records with the Report on Physical Count of Inventories and PPE to be able to reflect a complete and accurate balances in the financial statements.
36) The management shall likewise include in their yearly plan the conduct of the required physical count of Inventories and PPE to be undertaken by the agency’s Inventory Committee, and so required reports can be prepared and submitted to COA on or before January 31 of the ensuing year.
37) Invite representative from the Commission on Audit (COA) to act as witness in every inventory-taking to comply with the regulation. Invitation letter shall be served and stamped received by COA at least three days prior to the scheduled date to give the auditor an ample time to prepare and settle whatever time inconveniences that may have.

38) The management shall faithfully follow correct disbursement system and so only valid and proper claims will be processed and paid.
39) Exert all possible efforts to settle all audit suspensions within the required timeframe to avoid disallowance in audit, and the required ocular inspection could be promptly undertaken as well.
40) Be guided of the pertinent sections in the afore-mentioned regulations and in other GPPB issued resolutions simplifying the requirements in project implementation during emergency cases.

41) Exert all possible efforts to collate all the lacking documents and/or prepare a written justification for the non-settlement thereof, and submit the same to COA for the necessary evaluation and review, otherwise, the transactions covered by it will be disallowed in audit. Refrain from undertaking a project not supported with complete bid and technical documentation. However, awarding of contract shall only be made to a legally, financially and technically capable supplier/contractor and willing to cooperate in the submission of required document.
42) Assign additional qualified personnel to take charge in the preparation of documents and settlement of all audit suspensions to be able to meet the required deadlines.

43) The management shall prioritize finalization of the Municipality’s Solid Waste Management Plan and so submission of the same to the National Solid Waste Commission for approval could be undertaken.
44) Ensure that implementation of the related solid waste management programs/projects are authorized programs and projects included in the approved plan, as any deviation from the said plan may pose doubts in the said implementation and could be a ground for disallowance in audit.
45) Complete other required documentation/data on Solid Waste Management and immediately submit copy of the same to COA including the creation of the Solid Waste Management Board and proof in the establishment of the required recovery and treatment facilities.
46) Fully comply with the Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 and other regulations pertaining thereto to be able to have a systematic and comprehensive ecological waste management program.

47) The Municipal Treasurer shall coordinate with the Municipal Assessor in the preparation of the required Certified List of Taxpayers, and immediately provide copy of the same to the Municipal Accountant to be able to establish accurate amount of basic RPT and SET Receivables.
48) Upon receipt of the latest Certified List of Taxpayers, the Municipal Accountant shall make necessary adjusting entries to correct the recorded amount of RPT/SET Receivable vis a vis the submitted certified list of taxpayers.
49) The accounting office shall likewise maintain an index card for each taxpayer to facilitate the establishment of the accurate amount of collectibles from each taxpayer.
50) Regularly update RPT/SET Receivable accounts Subsidiary Ledgers.
51) We recommended to management to adhere with the afore-mentioned regulation on the releases of barangay shares which is within five (5) days after the end of each quarter.

52) Concerned offices shall give extra effort and prepare individual PPMPs correctly and within the deadline.
53) All procurement requirements shall be included in the prepared PPMPs including those needed in the construction/repair of agency’s programs and projects, and in various GAD and LDRRM undertakings. PPMPs shall be prepared annually and submit the same to the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) immediately thereafter for consolidation into Annual Procurement Plan (APP) using the prescribed format as required in the above-mentioned law and regulations. The approved APP shall serve as the control in any agency’s procurement; hence, no procurement shall be undertaken unless it is in accordance with the said plan.

54) Municipal Budget Officer shall always take heed to the requirement of the law and ensure that budget records were maintained and updated regularly to be able to provide reliable information towards fair presentation of agency’s Financial Statements.

55) Reconcile discrepancy noted as per the submitted copy of the RPT Declaration and other ownership documents with the book balance of the Land Account in the financial statements, and make necessary journal entries to correct errors made following the prescribed valuation in PPSAS 17.
56) Identify those lots recorded in the books without the required legal documents of ownership as those should be properly disclosed in the notes to Financial Statements.
57) Concerned officials shall continue to work on the titling of the afore-mentioned lots to be able to provide proof of ownership and so the interest of the municipality on the properties are secured, and likewise, ensures the legitimacy of recording in the books.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended that management shall undertake the following:
- The head of agency shall direct concerned official to limit payment thru
reimbursement basis and comply with the requirement on the principle of
imprest system; that all payment must be paid by check except those that
cannot be conveniently paid by check, it could be paid thru the cash advance
of the Municipal Treasurer.
- Procurement of office supplies and other recurring expenses of the
municipality shall be undertaken in bulk for a maximum of 3 months
consumption thru procurement service/public bidding or even thru shopping
so as to minimize transactions thru reimbursements.
- Practice of recording reimbursements in lieu of cash advances and/or vice
versa shall be discontinued as this manifestly show the motive of
circumventing the law since the payee could not be granted cash advance
other than official travel and/or no supporting documents is available to
justify the claim thru reimbursement.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended that management shall undertake the following:
- Ensure that all procurements shall pass through the regular mode of
procurement to promote fair competition in accordance with R.A. 9184.
Comply with the requirements on adequate procurement planning to
forestall emergency purchases.
- Procurement of office supplies and other recurring expenses of the
Municipality shall be undertaken in bulk for a maximum of 3 months
consumption thru procurement service/public bidding or even thru shopping
so as to minimize transactions thru reimbursements.
- Submit a justification letter explaining therein the reasons of the said claims
despite violation of existing regulation.
- Municipal Accountant shall likewise ensure that all disbursements are
supported with complete documentation.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended the following:
- Existing practice of the agency on processing claims not duly supported with
documents shall be discontinued as this is not the proper way of processing
claims and it may further create adverse impact to agency and to concerned
officials if this will continue to exist in their transactions.
- Submit a written justification explaining therein the reasons for processing
claims without complete documentation.
- The Municipal Accountant shall likewise submit all the required
documentation needed to establish the validity and legality of the
disbursements and henceforth, payment shall only be made for claims
supported with complete documentation.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended the following:
- The Municipal Treasurer shall render her monthly reports such as
Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) with supporting documents, Payrolls,
Official Receipts and other liquidation documents including revenue reports
and supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant on or before the 5th
day of the ensuing month to allow its prompt recording in the books of
accounts.
- The Municipal Accountant shall coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to
ensure prompt submission of DVs and other related reports, undertake
recording in the books of accounts, and submit the same within 10 days of
the ensuing month to the Office of the Auditor.
- Concerned officials shall prioritize and adopt measures to address issues on
delayed submission of reports to be able to meet the deadline set and be free
from possible sanctions may be imposed from violating thereof as required
under article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.
- Require concerned official/s to submit written justification explaining
therein the reasons for late submission, and if not satisfied, appropriate
actions shall be instituted against the accountable officers for the delayed/non
submission of financial reports as required under Article 218 of the Revised
Penal Code.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended to management the following:
- To faithfully comply and enforce the periodic submission of these reports
within the prescribed period. Concerned officials shall likewise prioritize
submission of these reports and look into the root cause of these delays and
immediately initiate measures to address the problem.
- Require concern official/s to submit written justification explaining therein
the reasons of late submission, and if not satisfied, appropriate actions shall
be instituted against the accountable officers for the delayed/non
submission of financial reports as required under Article 218 of the Revised
Penal Code.

Recommendation:
We hereby recommended that management shall cause the preparation and
submission of the required Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly
Report of Fuel Consumption to comply with the foregoing regulations, and to
facilitate proper control and accounting of the fuel expense; and establish
reasonableness of fuel consumption as well.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended the following:
- The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer shall exert
more efforts to maintain their respective accounting and budget records,
using the prescribed form, and update the same regularly.
- The necessity of additional workforce, who has the required expertise and
qualification, for the said offices shall be properly considered and shall be
prioritized as well including their attendance to trainings/seminars to keep
them abreast of the latest accounting and budget issuances.

- Invitation shall be given at least three days prior to the scheduled date to

DRRM Equipment ( 1-07-05-090) P300,000.00

The management shall undertake the following:
- The Municipal Accountant shall not process claims/payments not duly
supported with required documents. He shall likewise follow correct
disbursement system at all times.
- Exert all possible efforts to settle all audit suspensions within the required
timeframe to avoid disallowance in audit.
- Be guided of the pertinent sections in the afore-mentioned regulations and in
other GPPB issued resolutions simplifying the requirements in project
implementation during emergency cases.

We hereby recommended the following:
- The Municipal Accountant shall prioritize submission of the COVID related
reports and transactions documents since the prescribed period within which
to submit has already lapsed, and the necessity of its immediate audit has
been laid down to establish proper utilization thereof.

We hereby recommended the following:
- The Accounting, Supply and Property Units shall update regularly, PPE
Subsidiary Ledgers, Stock Cards and Property Cards, respectively, and
reconcile balances of these records with the Report on Physical Count of
Inventories and PPE to be able to reflect a complete and accurate balances in
the financial statements.
- The management shall likewise include in their yearly plan the conduct of the
required physical count of Inventories and PPE to be undertaken by the
agency’s Inventory Committee, and so required reports can be prepared and
submitted to COA on or before January 31 of the ensuing year.
- Invite representative from the Commission on Audit (COA) to act as witness
in every inventory-taking to comply with the regulation. Invitation letter
shall be served and stamped received by COA at least three days prior to the scheduled
date to give the auditor an ample time to prepare and settle whatever time
inconveniences that may have.

We hereby recommended the following:
- The management shall faithfully follow correct disbursement system and so
only valid and proper claims will be processed and paid.
- Exert all possible efforts to settle all audit suspensions within the required
timeframe to avoid disallowance in audit, and the required ocular inspection
could be promptly undertaken as well.
- Be guided of the pertinent sections in the afore-mentioned regulations and
in other GPPB issued resolutions simplifying the requirements in project
implementation during emergency cases.

We recommended the following:
- Prioritize submission of the monthly report of transactions to COA within
the deadline set to avoid negative repercussion; and timely audit of its
transactions could be undertaken as well.
- Maximize work assignment among accounting personnel and/or assign
additional staff, if the present workforce is not enough, so as to prevent work
overload and meet deadlines on reports submission.

The management shall undertake the following:
- Exert all possible efforts to collate all the lacking documents and/or prepare
a written justification for the non-settlement thereof, and submit the same to
COA for the necessary evaluation and review, otherwise, the transactions
covered by it will be disallowed in audit.
- Refrain from undertaking a project not supported with complete bid and
technical documentation. However, awarding of contract shall only be made
to a legally, financially and technically capable supplier/contractor and
willing to cooperate in the submission of required documents.
- Assign additional qualified personnel to take charge in the preparation of
documents and settlement of all audit suspensions to be able to meet the
required deadlines.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer shall stamp “PAID” all DVs
and its supporting documents after payment was made, to prevent the re-use of
the same on similar transaction.

We hereby recommended to management the following:
- The Municipal Accountant ensures that the required supporting documents
of all claims are complete before affixing her/his signature in Box A of the
Disbursement Voucher.
- The management shall likewise observe that proper flow of transactions is
strictly followed so that only valid and proper claims are processed and
paid.

We hereby recommended the following:
- The management shall prioritize finalization of the Municipality’s Solid
Waste Management Plan and so submission of the same to the National Solid
Waste Commission for approval could be undertaken.
- Ensure that implementation of the related solid waste management
programs/projects are authorized programs and projects included in the
approved plan, as any deviation from the said plan may pose doubts in the
said implementation and could be a ground for disallowance in audit.
- Complete other required documentation/data on Solid Waste Management
and immediately submit copy of the same to COA including the creation of
the Solid Waste Management Board and proof in the establishment of the
required recovery and treatment facilities.
- Fully comply with the Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 and other
regulations pertaining thereto to be able to have a systematic and
comprehensive ecological waste management program.

- Observe deadlines in reports submission for a timely audit and inspection,

regulations pertaining thereto to be able to have a systematic and
comprehensive ecological waste management program.

preparation of the required Certified List of Taxpayers, and immediately
provide copy of the same to the Municipal Accountant to be able to establish
accurate amount of basic RPT and SET receivables.

Accountant shall make necessary adjusting entries to correct the recorded
amount of RPT/SET Receivable vis a vis the submitted certified list of
taxpayers.

to facilitate the establishment of the accurate amount of collectibles from
each taxpayer.

regulation on the releases of barangay shares which is within five (5) days after
the end of each quarter.

- Discontinue payment of claims not supported with complete
documentations.

timeframe to avoid disallowance in audit.
- Assign additional qualified personnel to take charge in the settlement of all
audit suspensions to be able to meet the required deadlines.

- Head of Agency shall request and/or demand in writing from the Municipal
Treasurer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their transactions,
and submit the same together with other supporting documents to the
Accounting Section within the time prescribed by the regulation.
- Head of the accounting section shall prioritize submission of transaction
documents and other financial reports to COA and maximize work
assignment among accounting personnel so as to prevent work overload and
meet deadlines on reports submission.
- Take heed to the prescribed timeframe on submission of accounts and
financial reports to avoid sanctions that may be imposed from violating
thereof as required under section 127 of P.D. 1445, which provides that:
Administrative disciplinary actions:
Subject to rules and regulations as may be approved by the president,
any unjustified failure by the public officer concerned to comply with
any requirement imposed in this Code shall constitute neglect of duty
and shall be a ground for administrative disciplinary action against
the said public officer who, with the degree of his guilt in accordance
with the civil service law. Repeated unjustified failure to comply with
the requirements impose in this Code shall be conclusive proof the
public officer concerned is notoriously undesirable.

- Concerned offices shall give extra effort and prepare individual PPMPs
correctly and within the deadline.
- All procurement requirements shall be included in the prepared PPMPs
including those needed in the construction/repair of agency’s programs and
projects, and in various GAD and LDRRM undertakings.
- PPMPs shall be prepared annually and submit the same to the Bids and
Awards Committee (BAC) immediately thereafter for consolidation into
Annual Procurement Plan (APP) using the prescribed format. This approved
APP shall serve as the control in any agency’s procurement; hence, no
procurement shall be undertaken unless it is in accordance with the said
plan.

We hereby recommended that management shall instruct concerned official to
submit immediately all the required documentation relative to its proper
implementation together with the breakdown of its actual expenditures and
other necessary data to facilitate the conduct of our post-audit activity.

- Fully implement budgeted amount for GAD related programs and
activities to be able to meet its minimum requirement.
- The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer shall discontinue
processing of claims/payments for GAD related projects and activities
without appropriate documentation so as to avoid audit suspension and
disallowance.

The management shall undertake the following:
- Comply with the required laws, rules and regulations on LDRRM to
avoid sanction that may be imposed from violating thereof.
- The Municipal Budget Officer shall prepare and maintain separate
LDRRM Registries of Appropriation, Allotments and Obligations for
QRF, MOOE and Capital Outlay as required in the afore-mentioned
regulation.
- The Municipal Accountant shall also prepare and maintain subsidiary
records for LDRRM and make it available to COA for the necessary
review and verification; and provide adequate disclosure in the Notes to
Financial Statements.

Municipal Budget Officer shall always takes heed to the requirement of the law
and ensure that budget records were maintained and updated regularly to be
able to provide reliable information towards fair presentation of agency’s
Financial Statements.

- Prepare the required AAPSI within the deadline set, which is within 60 days
upon receipt of the Annual Audit Report, following the correct format and
incorporating therein all audit findings and recommendations stated in Part
II of the said report.
- Submit immediately the revised copy of the AAPSI for CY 2018 to enable us
validates its contents and prepare corresponding report as well.
- Provide copy of the said AAPSI to DBM, Speaker of the House of
Representatives, House Committee on Appropriations and Senate Committee
on Finance as mandated in section 99 of R.A. 11260.
- Posting of the said report in the agency’s website is likewise enjoined.

We recommended to management to undertake the following:
- Reconcile discrepancy noted as per the submitted copy of the RPT
Declaration and other ownership documents with the book balance of the
Land Account in the financial statements, and make necessary journal
entries to correct errors made following the prescribed valuation in PPSAS
17.
- Identify those lots recorded in the books without the required legal
documents of ownership as those should be properly disclosed in the notes
to Financial Statements.
- Concerned officials shall continue to work on the titling of the afore-
mentioned lots to be able to provide proof of ownership and so the interest
of the municipality on the properties is secured, and likewise ensures the
legitimacy of recording in the books.
- Appropriate enough funds for the titling or securing proof of ownership of
these parcels of land.
- Provide us details of the land account recorded in the books including copy
of its updated Subsidiary Ledger (SL).

We recommended the following, viz:
- The Municipal Accountant shall provide depreciation expense in all their
Infrastructure Assets.
- Make necessary adjusting entries to correct balances of the affected
accounts.
- Compute depreciation expense and the related accumulated depreciation
following the afore-mentioned regulations.
- Revisit PPSAS 17 and other pertinent COA regulations, which prescribed
accounting treatment for PPE, to keep you guided in the recognition, de-
recognition and depreciation of related PPE accounts.
- Provide us copy of the updated Subsidiary Ledgers (SL) of all PPE
accounts and its Accumulated Depreciation.

- Formulate Solid Waste Management (SWM) Plan and have it approved
by the National Solid Waste Management Commission to ensure that
programs, projects and activities included in the afore-mentioned plan
are in accordance with the National Solid Waste Management
Framework.
- Complete required documentation/data on Solid Waste Management and
submit copy to COA immediately afterwards.
- Solid waste management programs/projects must align with the approved
SWM Plan. Any deviation from the said plan could be a ground for
disallowance in audit.
- Fully comply with the Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 and other
regulations pertaining thereto to be able to have a systematic and
comprehensive ecological waste management program.

- Refrain from processing claims and payments for Local Development
Projects without the pre-implementation requirements and other required
documents so as to avoid audit suspension and disallowance.
- Instruction shall be given to concerned officials to immediately settle audit
suspensions and so technical documentation can be forwarded to COA
inspectorate team for the necessary inspection and validation.
- Submit all the required documentation for inspection and validation
immediately and within the deadline for a timely inclusion of its results in
the Annual Audit report.

We hereby recommended to management the following:
- The Municipal Mayor shall require the Municipal Engineer and all
department heads/end users to submit their respective PPMP regularly as
this will be used as basis by the BAC in the preparation of APP, and
furnish copy to the office of the auditor immediately afterwards.
- The BAC shall include in the agency Annual Procurement Plan (APP) all
procurement requirements either for infrastructure projects or
construction materials for projects undertaken by administration
including those needed for the implementation of various programs and
activities to be able to present a complete and valid procurement plan.
Incomplete APP could be a ground for declaring a null and void
procurement activity, thus, should be disallowed in audit.
- Use the APP format as prescribed by the GPPB and update the same
regularly to suit the needs of the agency’s procurement.
- Strictly observe that no procurement shall be authorized and undertaken
unless it is in accordance with the approved APP

We recommended to management to adopt the following measures:
- Review and adopt systematic procedures in the processing of
claims/disbursements that will provide a check and balance of functions
and accountabilities.
- Assign qualified and efficient staff to assist the present workforce in
accounting unit and in other agency’s key offices so that reports can be
prepared promptly and within deadlines.
- Require payees to submit all the required documentation before payment.
- Strictly comply with the prescribed timeframe of submission to avoid
sanctions that may be imposed for violating thereof as required under
section 127 of P.D. 1445, which provides that:
Administrative disciplinary actions:
Subject to rules and regulations as may be approved by the
president, any unjustified failure by the public officer concerned to
comply with any requirement imposed in this Code shall constitute
neglect of duty and shall be a ground for administrative
disciplinary action against the said public officer who, with the
degree of his guilt in accordance with the civil service law.
Repeated unjustified failure to comply with the requirements
impose in this Code shall be conclusive proof that public officer
concerned is notoriously undesirable.

We hereby recommended to the Municipal Mayor and to other offices concerned
to strictly adhere to the Revised IRR of RA 9184 in all their procurement
activities. Moreover, immediately settle all other related audit suspensions,
otherwise, we will be compelled to issue notice of disallowance on items covered
by the said suspensions.

issue certification stating therein that previous cash advance has been liquidated

view of generating timely and relevant audit results.

We recommended to management to require supplier/contractor to post
